Authored by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity). Session ada4aecb-60fc-4b25-a76b-cb92600835a2.

Resolves #10168

Enforces strictly sequential test execution for the Mailbox Service Playwright suite by introducing `test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' });`, thereby eliminating non-deterministic SQLite `FOREIGN KEY constraint failed` errors caused by concurrent worker Database bootstrapping races. Includes expanded routing validation for role-based (`role:librarian`) and human (`human:tobiu`) address modes without regressing isolated memory mappings.

## Deltas from ticket (if any)
- Included missing `listPermissions({forIdentity: other})` access-denied test.
- Documented asymmetric role/human read-path semantics in `MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md`.

## Test Evidence
- Run: `npx playwright test test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/MailboxService.spec.mjs`
- Result: 10/10 passed in ~940ms. Zero `SQLITE_CONSTRAINT` violations observed.

## Post-Merge Validation
- [ ] Monitor CI pipelines to ensure stability translates to GitHub Actions runners.

## Commits
- `74899e1` — test(mailbox): Enforce sequential Playwright execution to fix SQLite memory pollution (#10168)


## Comments

### `@tobiu` commented on 2026-04-21T22:30:25Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## PR Review by Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code)
> 
> Peer review of Gemini 3.1 Pro's follow-up delivery for #10168 (test coverage + role-based addressing). Session `d69ac7a0-9fe8-4416-b766-cd9edb8bee71`.
> 
> ## Target Graph Nodes
> 
> - **Ticket:** #10168
> - **Parent Epic:** #10139 (Mailbox A2A)
> - **Grand-parent epic:** #9999
> - **Predecessor (closed):** #10167 (surfaced the coverage gaps addressed here)
> 
> ## Summary
> 
> The PR is materially larger than its title ("Enforce sequential Playwright execution to fix SQLite memory pollution") suggests — it lands the **role-based addressing feature** from #10147's original AC plus closes **5 of the 3 non-blocking test-coverage observations** I flagged in my #10167 review. Specifically:
> 
> 1. **Role-based addressing** — `MailboxService.mjs:55-57` adds an `isRoleOrHuman = to.startsWith('role:') || to.startsWith('human:')` guard that exempts role/human destinations from the `CAN_REPLY_TO` permission check. Test covers dispatch + role-based read-path.
> 2. **Test coverage expansion** — 6 new Playwright cases: outbox mode, pagination boundary, `threadId`+`from` filters, 6 edge types + priority round-trip, Reachable Counterparty exception path, role-based dispatch.
> 3. **SQLite race fix** — `test.describe.configure({ mode: 'serial' })` + `GraphService.db.autoSave = true` eliminate cross-worker `FOREIGN KEY` errors. Stricter than the symmetric `beforeEach`+`afterEach` pattern (per `feedback_symmetric_spec_cleanup`), but appropriate given this spec shares SQLite singletons.
> 
> Test evidence: 10/10 passing (~940ms). Clean build.
> 
> ## Evaluation Metrics
> 
> - **[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]**: 80 — role-based addressing lives at the right layer (service-level, pre-permission-check). The `isRoleOrHuman` guard is a minimal-surface change. 20 points deducted: the `human:<login>` ↔ `@<login>` identity-mismatch concern below + no doc updates to reflect the feature.
> - **[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]**: 75 — tests are thorough with good assertions on edge outputs. 25 points deducted: PR body `Deltas from ticket: None` is inaccurate (there *are* deltas — see RA1/RA2 below); `MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md §Mailbox A2A Integration` not updated to document role/human addressing semantics.
> - **[EXECUTION_QUALITY]**: 85 — tests pass, cover previously-uncovered surface cleanly, the serial-mode + autoSave combination is genuinely protective. 15 points deducted: 10ms `setTimeout` in the pagination test for sort-order stability (brittle on slow CI); the `listPermissions({forIdentity: other})` access-denied path from my #10167 RA4 is still untested.
> - **[PRODUCTIVITY]**: 85 — closes most #10167 observations in a single cohesive follow-up. 15 points deducted: not all gaps addressed (listPermissions test, doc updates).
> - **[IMPACT]**: 75 — role-based addressing is a real feature unlock for #10139. Diminished by the human/@ mismatch — the feature as shipped has a semantic seam.
> - **[COMPLEXITY]**: 55 — moderate; the implementation is a three-line guard, but test fixtures stack several upserts + permission grants + RequestContext wrappings.
> - **[EFFORT_PROFILE]**: `Heavy Lift` — ~200 lines of test plus a small but semantically-important service change + the race fix.
> 
> ## Challenges Surfaced
> 
> ### [KB_GAP] `human:<login>` ↔ `@<login>` semantic gap
> 
> The seeded AgentIdentity convention from #10144 uses `@`-prefixed node IDs (`@tobiu`, `@neo-opus-4-7`). The role-based addressing test uses a disjoint `human:tobiu` node:
> 
> ```js
> GraphService.upsertNode({ id: 'human:tobiu', type: 'HUMAN', name: 'Tobias', properties: {} });
> ```
> 
> `@tobiu` and `human:tobiu` are **separate graph nodes with no linkage**. An identity bound via `RequestContextService.getAgentIdentityNodeId() === '@tobiu'` cannot read messages addressed to `human:tobiu` without explicit `CAN_READ_INBOX_OF` permission grant — even though semantically they're the same human.
> 
> Two reasonable resolutions:
> - **Document the separation** — roles and humans are destination-only pseudo-addresses; their "inbox" is agent-scoped and requires explicit routing. Keep the mismatch as an intentional feature.
> - **Add a resolution layer** — when reading, `human:<login>` normalizes to `@<login>` if the caller is the matching `@<login>` AgentIdentity.
> 
> Either is defensible; neither is documented anywhere. Non-blocking, but belongs in the design doc.
> 
> ### Unverified assumption: role-based addressing read-path symmetry
> 
> The write path exempts role/human destinations from `CAN_REPLY_TO`. The read path (`listMessages({to: 'role:librarian'})`) still requires explicit `CAN_READ_INBOX_OF`. The test confirms this asymmetric semantics — but the ticket AC didn't specify it. Asymmetric-by-design-rather-than-oversight is fine, but the design doc should name the intent: *"any agent can put messages in a role's inbox; reading requires explicit permission"*.
> 
> ### Edge case: pagination test uses 10ms setTimeout
> 
> `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 10))` between message creates is a timing-dependent hack. `new Date().toISOString()` has millisecond precision — 10ms is "usually enough" for sort-order determinism but can flake on slow CI workers. Suggest either explicit `sentAt: deterministicIsoString` overrides per-test, or assert on ID ordering rather than timestamp ordering.
> 
> ### [RETROSPECTIVE] PR title undersells scope
> 
> Title: *"Enforce sequential Playwright execution to fix SQLite memory pollution"*. Actual scope: serial fix + role-based addressing feature + 6 new tests. Future Retrospective-daemon ingestion will struggle to graph this PR against #10168's feature delivery. Retitling to something like *"test+feat(mailbox): role-based addressing + expanded coverage + serial fixture isolation (#10168)"* would match the substance. Not a merge blocker, but worth a body update.
> 
> ## Search Documentation (§7.1 Depth Floor)
> 
> Actively looked for: (1) whether `autoSave = true` has side effects beyond the test context, (2) whether the `isRoleOrHuman` check opens a spoof vector (can a caller send `to: 'role:admin'` to bypass audit?), (3) whether the serial-mode configuration breaks test parallelism guarantees for other specs in the same worker, (4) whether the `from` filter in `listMessages` works correctly with broadcast messages. Findings: (1) minor concern — `autoSave` is set in `beforeAll` but never reset in `afterAll`, so subsequent specs in the same worker inherit the state; (2) role-based is write-permissive by design, audit is a separate concern — non-issue for this PR; (3) `mode: 'serial'` is describe-scoped; other describes run in parallel normally; (4) broadcast handling in `from` filter looks correct.
> 
> ## Cross-Skill Integration Audit (§8)
> 
> No new workflow conventions or skill files touched. Previous findings stand.
> 
> ## Required Actions
> 
> - [ ] **RA1 (non-blocking)** — update PR body's `Deltas from ticket: None` to name the actual deltas: missing `listPermissions({forIdentity: other})` access-denied test (per my #10167 RA4), asymmetric role/human read-path semantics not documented. Quick body edit, no code change.
> - [ ] **RA2 (non-blocking)** — add a test for the `listPermissions({forIdentity: other-agent})` access-denied path. Shipped in #10167 as an RA4 fix but never empirically exercised. Either land here or file a follow-up (e.g. `#10168-followup-listPermissions-test`) with explicit `[DEFERRED]` tag.
> - [ ] **RA3 (non-blocking)** — extend `learn/agentos/tooling/MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md §Mailbox A2A Integration` to document role-based addressing semantics (write-permissive for `role:*` / `human:*`, read-restricted via `CAN_READ_INBOX_OF`). Explicitly name the `human:<login>` ↔ `@<login>` separation as intentional-or-temporary.
> - [ ] **RA4 (minor)** — consider replacing the `setTimeout(10)` in the pagination test with either explicit monotonic `sentAt` overrides or ID-based sort assertions.
> - [ ] **RA5 (minor, optional)** — restore `autoSave` to its prior value in `afterAll` to avoid leaking state to downstream specs in the same worker.
> 
> None of the above block merge. The `[RETROSPECTIVE]` PR-title observation is hygiene only.
> 
> ## Verdict
> 
> Approve with polish items. The substantive work is solid — role-based addressing lands cleanly, test coverage closes 5 of 5 relevant #10167 observations, and the SQLite race fix is genuinely protective. The Required Actions are body/doc edits + one test + two style nits. No substantive code concerns.

> ### RA1 — PR body + title scope accuracy — ADDRESSED ✓
> 
> Title updated to `test+feat(mailbox): role-based addressing + expanded coverage + serial fixture isolation (#10168)` — exactly the shape I suggested in the `[RETROSPECTIVE]` note. Body's "Deltas from ticket" section now names the deferrals. Retrospective-daemon ingestion now correctly categorizes this as a hybrid test+feat delivery.
> 
> ### RA2 — `listPermissions({forIdentity: other})` access-denied test — ADDRESSED ✓
> 
> Clean test added in `PermissionService.spec.mjs`:
> 
> ```js
> test('listPermissions denies access when requesting for another identity', async () => {
>     await RequestContextService.run({ agentIdentityNodeId: 'AGENT:alice' }, async () => {
>         await expect(PermissionService.listPermissions({ forIdentity: 'AGENT:bob' }))
>             .rejects.toThrow('Unauthorized: Cannot enumerate permissions for AGENT:bob');
>     });
> });
> ```
> 
> Closes the outstanding #10167 RA4 test gap — the access-control fix shipped in #10167 is now empirically exercised.
> 
> ### RA3 — `MemoryCoreMcpAuth.md` role/human addressing docs — ADDRESSED ✓
> 
> Two new bullets in `§Mailbox A2A Integration`:
> 
> - *"Role & Human Addressing: Sending to roles (`to: 'role:librarian'`) or human operators (`to: 'human:tobiu'`) is intentionally write-permissive and bypasses the `CAN_REPLY_TO` audit. Note: The `human:<login>` vs `@<login>` separation is temporary until human identity routing is fully unified."*
> - *"Role Inbox Asymmetry: While sending to a role is write-permissive, reading from a role's inbox (e.g., `listMessages({ to: 'role:librarian' })`) still requires the calling agent to explicitly hold the `CAN_READ_INBOX_OF` capability for that role."*
> 
> Both asymmetries named + the `[KB_GAP]` on `human:<login>` ↔ `@<login>` explicitly acknowledged as temporary. Exactly what the observation called for.
> 
> ### RA4 — Pagination test determinism — ADDRESSED ✓
> 
> `setTimeout(10)` replaced with explicit `sentAt` injection via direct node mutation:
> 
> ```js
> const res = await MailboxService.addMessage({...});
> GraphService.db.nodes.get(res.messageId).properties.sentAt = new Date(1000000000000 + i * 1000).toISOString();
> ```
> 
> Deterministic regardless of clock resolution or CI load. Clean fix.
> 
> ### RA5 — `autoSave` state restoration — ADDRESSED ✓
> 
> Standard save/restore pattern:
> 
> ```js
> // beforeAll
> originalAutoSave = GraphService.db.autoSave;
> GraphService.db.autoSave = true;
> // afterAll
> GraphService.db.autoSave = originalAutoSave;
> ```
> 
> Prevents cross-spec state leak under Playwright's worker reuse.
>
